import json, os, sys, re, math
p="/mnt/data"
files=os.listdir(p)
files

qpath="/mnt/data/file-HHumqWkCbpLvx5yFcuNaEH-quranic_verses.json"
hpath="/mnt/data/file-GTpsjEG93EG6BAuDqWqgAJ-six_hadith_books.json"
with open(qpath,'r',encoding='utf-8') as f:
    q=json.load(f)
len(q)

target="وَعَدَدَ لَكُمْ فِعْلَ الشُكْرِ"
matches=[entry for entry in q if entry['ayah_text']==target]
len(matches)

def normalize_ar(text):
    import re
    # remove diacritics:
    tashkeel = "[\u0610-\u061A\u064B-\u0652\u0670\u06D6-\u06ED]"
    text = re.sub(tashkeel, "", text)
    # remove tatweel
    text = text.replace("\u0640","")
    # unify alef variants
    text = re.sub("[إأآا]", "ا", text)
    # unify hamza on waw/yeh? We'll just replace all hamza forms with ا maybe
    text = text.replace("ؤ","و").replace("ئ","ي").replace("ء","")
    # remove punctuation
    text = re.sub(r"[^\w\s\u0621-\u064A]", "", text)  # allow arabic letters and numbers and underscores?
    # collapse spaces
    text = re.sub(r"\s+"," ",text).strip()
    return text

# search for substring 'فِعْلَ الشُكْرِ'
for entry in q:
    if 'فِعْلَ' in entry['ayah_text'] or 'الشُكْرِ' in entry['ayah_text']:
        pass
# Let's look for normalized substring
substr_norm = normalize_ar("فِعْلَ الشُكْرِ")
candidates=[]
for entry in q:
    if substr_norm in normalize_ar(entry['ayah_text']):
        candidates.append(entry)
len(candidates)

sub="مَنَّ عَلَيْكُمْ"
for entry in q:

        print("found",entry)
        break
# normalized search
subn=normalize_ar(sub)
matches=[entry for entry in q if subn in normalize_ar(entry['ayah_text'])]
len(matches), matches[:5]

sub="لَقَدْ مَنَّ عَلَيْكُمْ رَبُّكُمْ"
normal_sub=normalize_ar(sub)
matches=[entry for entry in q if normal_sub in normalize_ar(entry['ayah_text'])]
len(matches)

def find_by_sub(sub):
    sn=normalize_ar(sub)
    res=[entry for entry in q if sn in normalize_ar(entry['ayah_text'])]
    return res

find_by_sub("وَعَدَ لَكُمْ")[:10], len(find_by_sub("وَعَدَ لَكُمْ"))
find_by_sub("وَعَدَ")[:20], len(find_by_sub("وَعَدَ"))

# search for occurrences of the substring 'شُكْر' in ayah_text
subs='شُكْر'
res=[entry for entry in q if subs in entry['ayah_text']]
len(res), res[:20]
find_by_sub("فِعْلَ")

import math, sys, re
target_full="وَعَدَدَ لَكُمْ فِعْلَ الشُكْرِ"
sn_target = normalize_ar(target_full)
tokens_target = sn_target.split()
def token_f1(a,b):
    # a and b are normalized strings
    ta=a.split()
    tb=b.split()
    setta=set(ta)
    settb=set(tb)
    # better to count multiset intersection
    from collections import Counter
    ca=Counter(ta); cb=Counter(tb)
    overlap=sum(min(ca[w], cb.get(w,0)) for w in ca)
    if overlap==0:
        return 0.0
    prec = overlap / sum(cb.values())
    rec = overlap / sum(ca.values())
    f1 = 2*prec*rec/(prec+rec)
    return f1

best=[]
for entry in q:
    norm=normalize_ar(entry['ayah_text'])
    f1=token_f1(sn_target,norm)
    best.append((f1, entry))
best_sorted=sorted(best, key=lambda x: x[0], reverse=True)
best_sorted[:10]
find_by_sub("مَنَّ"), find_by_sub("مَنَّ عَلَيْكُمْ")[:20]

with open(hpath,'r',encoding='utf-8') as f:
    had=json.load(f)
len(had)
had[:3]

# compute best token f1 match in hadith Matn and hadithTxt
best_h=(0,None,None) # (score,entry,field)
for entry in had:
    for fieldname in ('Matn','hadithTxt'):
        ftext=entry.get(fieldname)
        if not ftext: continue
        sn_f=normalize_ar(ftext)
        s=token_f1(sn_target, sn_f)
        if s>best_h[0]:
            best_h=(s, entry, fieldname)
best_h

# compute LCS length function
def lcs_len(a,b):
    # compute length of LCS for strings a,b (chars)
    la, lb = len(a), len(b)
    # use DP with only two rows
    prev = [0]*(lb+1)
    for i in range(1,la+1):
        cur = [0]*(lb+1)
        ai=a[i-1]
        for j in range(1,lb+1):
            if ai==b[j-1]:
                cur[j] = prev[j-1]+1
            else:
                cur[j] = prev[j] if prev[j]>=cur[j-1] else cur[j-1]
        prev = cur
    return prev[lb]

sn_target=normalize_ar(target_full)
len(sn_target), sn_target

best_q=(0,None)
for entry in q:
    s=normalize_ar(entry['ayah_text'])
    l=lcs_len(sn_target, s)
    ratio = l/len(sn_target)
    if ratio>best_q[0]:
        best_q=(ratio, entry)
best_q

# longest common substring length
def lcs_substr_len(a,b):
    # returns length of longest common substring
    la, lb = len(a), len(b)
    # DP table row optimization
    prev = [0]*(lb+1)
    maxl = 0
    for i in range(1, la+1):
        cur = [0]*(lb+1)
        ai = a[i-1]
        for j in range(1, lb+1):
            if ai == b[j-1]:
                cur[j] = prev[j-1] + 1
                if cur[j] > maxl:
                    maxl = cur[j]
            else:
                cur[j] = 0
        prev = cur
    return maxl

best_q_sub=(0,None)
for entry in q:
    s=normalize_ar(entry['ayah_text'])
    l=lcs_substr_len(sn_target, s)
    ratio = l/len(sn_target)
    if ratio>best_q_sub[0]:
        best_q_sub=(ratio, entry)
best_q_sub

# Levenshtein distance, with optimized implementation
def levenshtein(a,b):
    # a,b are strings
    if a==b:
        return 0
    la=len(a); lb=len(b)
    if la==0:
        return lb
    if lb==0:
        return la
    # ensure la<=lb to use less memory? We'll just implement standard DP
    # use two rows
    if la>lb:
        a,b=b,a
        la,lb = lb,la
    prev = list(range(lb+1))
    for i in range(1, la+1):
        cur = [i] + [0]*lb
        ai=a[i-1]
        for j in range(1, lb+1):
            cost = 0 if ai==b[j-1] else 1
            cur[j] = min(prev[j]+1, cur[j-1]+1, prev[j-1]+cost)
        prev = cur
    return prev[lb]

# compute best levenshtein similarity ratio across q: sim = 1 - dist/max(len(a),len(b))
best_lev_q=(0,None)
for entry in q:
    s=normalize_ar(entry['ayah_text'])
    dist=levenshtein(sn_target, s)
    maxlen=max(len(sn_target), len(s))
    sim = 1 - dist/maxlen if maxlen>0 else 1
    if sim>best_lev_q[0]:
        best_lev_q=(sim, entry)
best_lev_q
